texto: ...the Spirit comes to us as a fire, either to be fanned into full flame and given the freedom to accomplish his will or to be doused and extinguished by the water of human fear, control, and flawed theology.

seo.titleSeo: "...the Spirit comes to us as a fire, either to be fa…" — Sam Storms | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Sam Storms sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “...the Spirit comes to us as a fire, either to be fanned into full flame and given the freedom to……

frase_original: ...the Spirit comes to us as a fire, either to be fanned into full flame and given the freedom to accomplish his will or to be doused and extinguished by the water of human fear, control, and flawed theology.

texto: ...we must be careful to avoid the error of reductionism, as if the whole of the Spirit’s ministry can be reduced to Christology, as if the Spirit does nothing but glorify Christ. It’s the mistake of arguing that the primary purpose of the Spirit’s coming is the sole purpose of his coming. The principal aim of the Spirit in what he does is to awaken us to the glory, splendor, and centrality of the work of Christ Jesus. But this does not mean that it is less than the Spirit at work when he awakens us also to his own glory and power and abiding presence.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sam Storms: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “...we must be careful to avoid the error of reductionism, as if the whole of the Spirit’s ministr…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, christology, holy-spirit pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e trabalho.
